subject: Refactor policy/api csit tests
commit_message:
Refactor policy/api csit tests

* Removed the deprecated methods.
* Removed duplicated code.
* Created method hierarchy to improve the readability.
* Updated the assertions on various tests.

Issue-ID: POLICY-3257
Change-Id: Id68bf50b397f9aff594268b3ce508858a726328d
Signed-off-by: Ram Krishna Verma <ram_krishna.verma@bell.ca>

Patch Set 1:

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-api-master-merge-java/171/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-api-master-merge-java/171

Patch Set 1:

> Ahh! you are correct, the one that ran was master verify. I remember we use to have it earlier. Did we stopped keeping them?

Unfortunately, with the restructuring of the CSITs, it became more difficult to do a verify, as the master script in docker/csit only pulls the head of the branch for the repo under test.  Furthermore, the test requires a docker image of the repo to have been built and pushed to dockerio/nexus.

Because of the above, I'm wondering at the value of having each repo's CSIT kept within the repo instead of in the docker/csit.  It was thought that we'd update the CSITs at the same time as making other changes to the repo, but not sure that's really all that likely.  In addition, because it also causes all of the above issues, maybe we should move it all back to docker/csit, then we could reinstitute CSIT verifies, as well.

In addition, because it also causes all of the above issues, maybe we should move it all back to docker/csit, then we could reinstitute CSIT verifies, as well.

Thanks Jim! Good to know the history. I agree in bringing them to policy/docker and have the ability to run verify jobs on the reviews.
